title: Iowa: Iowa Consumer Data Protection Act (ICDPA) — narrower rights
summary: Iowa residents can opt out of the sale of personal data and request deletion of data they provided to a business. Iowa's law is among the narrowest of the state privacy laws: deletion is limited to data you gave the business, there is no standalone opt-out of targeted advertising, and no universal opt-out signal. The ICDPA took effect on January 1, 2025.
disclaimer: General information, not legal advice, and not yet independently reviewed. Because Iowa's rights are limited, the broker's own opt-out (below) is often the more effective route — verify before relying on either.
